rationale: Severe mismatch across the board: high-intensity anime combat with shouted VO and beam attacks contradicts Sand Loop's calm/ASMR sorting fantasy, and the only thread of overlap is incidental palette (blue/yellow). The challenge hook ("Try and survive this!") sets up combat skill expectations the core loop of tapping buckets and color-sorting cannot deliver, guaranteeing post-install churn. Audience skews young male shōnen fans rather than the 25-45 casual satisfying-puzzle demo — do not adapt this archetype; if a "challenge" angle is needed, build a fail-state/near-miss sand-spill version instead.
